evidence: During the fiscal years ended June 30, 2025, 2024 and 2023, we invested approximately $1.388 billion, $1.276 billion, and $1.195 billion, respectively, in research and development... including investments in generative AI
we extended AI tools to all of our product developers for coding, testing, and documentation
we continued deploying AI tools in our products and across our sales, service, and research and development functions
ADP Assist validates payroll information, checking for payroll anomalies and using generative AI to identify and help resolve missing tax registrations
ADP Assist uses generative AI to simplify report creation, helping HR practitioners access internal, national, and global workforce data to analyze compensation, turnover, candidate profile relevancy
we continue to extend generative AI capabilities to a broader portion of our service and implementation associates to deliver an even better client experience
if the data used to train a model or the model's output is inaccurate or biased, or alleged to be inaccurate or biased, we could be subject to reputational damage or litigation
Our use of generative AI in our products and operations also introduces additional risks, including risks related to accuracy, bias, transparency, security, and privacy

as we expand our use of AI, there is uncertainty regarding intellectual property ownership and license rights of AI algorithms and content generated by AI and we may become subject to similar claims of infringement

capex_mentions.quote: invested approximately $1.388 billion, $1.276 billion, and $1.195 billion, respectively, in research and development
capex_mentions.context: R&D spend for fiscal years 2025, 2024, 2023 respectively; explicitly includes investments in generative AI

limitations: R&D figures are total R&D, not AI-only; AI-specific spend is not separately quantified, reducing precision of ai_investment_intensity score

ai_narrative_concreteness scored 4 rather than 5 because no quantified business results (%, $, time saved) are disclosed for deployed AI use cases; awards and product descriptions are present but outcomes are not measured

ai_risk_disclosure_depth scored 4 because risk mechanisms are described with specificity (bias leading to litigation, IP uncertainty, regulatory cost) but no quantified risk exposure or named oversight body with formal governance charter is disclosed beyond the AI Data Ethics Committee mention
